operating_state: Goldman Sachs generated net revenues of $53.51 billion for 2024, 16% higher than 2023, driven by significantly higher investment banking revenues from leveraged finance and equity underwriting plus increased FICC and Equities revenues. Global Banking & Markets and Asset & Wealth Management delivered strong performance from higher management fees and equity investment gains. Daily VaR-based net revenues exceeded $100 million on 62 business days versus 52 in 2023.
cost_structure: Operating expenses decreased 2% to $33.77 billion, improving the efficiency ratio to 63.1% from 74.6% in 2023, primarily due to elimination of prior year charges including GreenSky write-downs and Consumer platforms goodwill impairment. Compensation and benefits expenses increased reflecting improved performance while technology investments in AI, distributed ledger systems, and electronic trading platforms require ongoing material resource allocation alongside escalating compliance and cybersecurity costs.
material_events: Goldman returned $11.80 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends while completing the GreenSky sale and entering agreements to transition the GM credit card program by Q3 2025. The firm formed Capital Solutions Group and achieved over $10 billion annual firmwide management fees. The Federal Reserve increased the stress capital buffer from 5.5% to 6.2% effective October 2024, while the G-SIB surcharge is expected to increase from 3.0% to 3.5% beginning in 2026.
macro_exposures: The firm faces broad macroeconomic concerns including inflation uncertainty, prolonged geopolitical stresses from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts, escalating U.S.-China tensions, and central bank policy uncertainty. Commercial real estate market concerns persist while interest rate sensitivity shows asymmetric exposure with earnings-at-risk of negative $270 million for 100 basis point rate decline versus positive $140 million for increases. Climate-related risks encompass both physical and transition risks from policy changes.
forward_outlook: Management targets through-the-cycle ROE of 14-16% and ROTE of 15-17%, with 2024 achieving 12.7% ROE and 13.5% ROTE, while expecting 2025 annual effective tax rate of approximately 21%. The firm targets growing total credit alternative assets to $300 billion by end of 2028 and selling down the vast majority of historical principal investments portfolio by end of 2026 while maintaining pre-tax breakeven for Platform Solutions by end of 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Credit rating downgrades could trigger additional collateral requirements of $315 million for one-notch and $1.20 billion for two-notch downgrades. The firm maintains $2.16 billion in unrecognized tax benefits and faces potential material impact from OECD Pillar II minimum tax legislation while legal and regulatory proceedings carry estimated liabilities with potential aggregate losses exceeding reserves. Cybersecurity risks have intensified with AI-enabled fraud and ransomware attacks requiring ongoing resource allocation.
